title: Water-resistant and durable biocarbon pellets, and methods for making and using the same
abstract: Water-resistant, high-durability, and high-density biocarbon pellets are described. In some variations, a process for producing biocarbon pellets comprises: pyrolyzing a biomass-containing feedstock in a pyrolysis reactor, thereby generating a solid biocarbon-containing material and a pyrolysis vapor; introducing the pyrolysis vapor to a separation system configured to recover a pyrolysis condensate in liquid form separated from non-condensable gases; capturing pyrolysis condensate as a polyphenolic material; contacting the solid biocarbon-containing material with the polyphenolic material, thereby generating an intermediate mixture; introducing the intermediate mixture to a densification unit, thereby generating a biocarbon pellet precursor; and at least partially drying the biocarbon pellet precursor, thereby generating biocarbon pellets characterized by a 24-hour water uptake of about 20 wt% or less, measured at 25Â°C and 1 bar. The biocarbon pellets can be stockpiled outside, which is logistically convenient at manufacturing sites, such as industrial metal-making plants that feed the biocarbon pellets into the process.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS I/We claim: 1 . A process for producing a biocarbon pellet, the process comprising: (a) pyrolyzing a biomass-containing feedstock in a pyrolysis reactor, thereby generating a solid biocarbon-containing material and a pyrolysis vapor that contains a polyphenolic material; (b) introducing the pyrolysis vapor to a thermally controlled multiple-stage separation system configured to separate a pyrolysis condensate in liquid form from non-condensable gases, wherein the pyrolysis condensate contains the polyphenolic material; (c) contacting the solid biocarbon-containing material with the polyphenolic material, thereby generating an intermediate mixture; (d) introducing the intermediate mixture to a densification unit, thereby generating a biocarbon pellet precursor; and (e) at least partially drying the biocarbon pellet precursor, thereby generating a biocarbon pellet, wherein the biocarbon pellet is characterized by a 24-hour water uptake of about 20 wt% or less, measured at 25Â°C and 1 bar. 2. The process of claim 1 , wherein the biomass-containing feedstock is selected from softwood chips, hardwood chips, timber harvesting residue, tree branches, tree stumps, leaves, bark, sawdust, corn, corn stover, wheat, wheat straw, rice, rice straw, sugarcane, sugarcane bagasse, sugarcane straw, energy cane, sugar beets, sugar beet pulp, sunflowers, sorghum, canola, algae, miscanthus, alfalfa, switchgrass, fruits, fruit shells, fruit stalks, fruit peels, fruit pits, vegetables, vegetable shells, vegetable stalks, vegetable peels, vegetable pits, grape pumice, almond shells, pecan shells, coconut shells, coffee grounds, food waste, commercial waste, grass pellets, hay pellets, wood pellets, cardboard, paper, paper pulp, paper packaging, paper trimmings, food packaging, construction or demolition waste, railroad ties, lignin, animal manure, municipal solid waste, municipal sewage, or a combination thereof. 3. The process of claim 1 , wherein the solid biocarbon-containing material contains from about 65 wt% to about 95 wt% fixed carbon. 4. The process of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the thermally controlled multiple-stage separation system includes one or more filters, absorption beds with solid media, absorption beds with liquid media, adsorption beds with solid media, dry scrubbers, wet scrubbers, Venturi scrubbers, centrifuges, cyclones, quench units, reactive condensers, distillation columns, reactive-distillation columns, evaporators, reactive evaporators, heat exchangers, fan separators, electrostatic precipitators, demisters, or a combination thereof, configured for generating the pyrolysis condensate and a gas stream from the pyrolysis vapor. 5. The process of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the thermally controlled multiple-stage separation system includes a cyclone unit configured for separating the solid biocarbon-containing material from the pyrolysis vapor. 6. The process of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein a quench unit is configured for exposing the pyrolysis vapor to water, to generate the pyrolysis condensate. 7. The process of claim 4, wherein the thermally controlled multiple-stage separation system further includes a demister unit configured to recover residual liquid from the gas stream. 8. The process of claim 7, wherein the residual liquid is combined with the pyrolysis condensate. 9. The process of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the thermally controlled multiple-stage separation system is configured to capture multiple liquid fractions. 10. The process of claim 9, wherein the thermally controlled multiple-stage separation system is configured to capture the multiple liquid fractions according to molecular weight, boiling point, polarity, or a combination thereof. 11. The process of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the polyphenolic material contains from about 20 wt% to about 80 wt% fixed carbon. 12. The process of any one of claims 1 to 11 , wherein the polyphenolic material has a weight-average molecular weight from about 75 g/mol to about 50,000 g/mol. 13. The process of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the polyphenolic material has a viscosity at 25Â°C from about 1 cP to about 1000 cP. 14. The process of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the polyphenolic material contains from about 1 wt% to about 60 wt% water. 15. The process of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the intermediate mixture has a weight ratio of the polyphenolic material to the solid biocarbon-containing material selected from about 0.01 to about 5. 16. The process of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the polyphenolic material is heated prior to contacting with the solid biocarbon-containing material. 17. The process of claim 16, wherein the polyphenolic material is heated to a temperature of at least about 150Â°C. 18. The process of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the solid biocarbon- containing material is heated prior to contacting with the polyphenolic material. 19. The process of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the solid biocarbon- containing material is at a temperature within Â±100Â°C of the temperature of the pyrolysis reactor, prior to contacting with the polyphenolic material. 20. The process of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the solid biocarbon- containing material is at a temperature within Â±50Â°C of the temperature of the pyrolysis reactor, prior to contacting with the polyphenolic material. 21 . The process of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the intermediate mixture is heated prior to step (d). 22. The process of any one of claims 1 to 21 , wherein the densification unit is selected from an extruder, a briquetter, a pellet mill, or a combination thereof. 23. The process of any one of claims 1 to 22, wherein the densification unit is operated at a densification temperature greater than 100Â°C. 24. The process of any one of claims 1 to 23, wherein in step (d), the densification unit is configured to produce an enriched concentration of polyphenolic material in an outer shell of the biocarbon pellet precursor. 25. The process of any one of claims 1 to 24, wherein steps (d) and (e) are combined, and wherein the densification unit is also configured for the drying of the biocarbon pellet precursor. 26. The process of any one of claims 1 to 25, wherein the biocarbon pellet has a moisture content from 0 wt% to about 10 wt% water. 27. The process of any one of claims 1 to 25, wherein the biocarbon pellet has a moisture content from 0 wt% to about 5 wt% water. 28. The process of any one of claims 1 to 25, wherein the biocarbon pellet has a moisture content from 0 wt% to about 2 wt% water. 29. The process of any one of claims 1 to 28, wherein the biocarbon pellet has a fixed-carbon content from about 50 wt% fixed carbon to about 99 wt% fixed carbon on a dry basis. 30. The process of any one of claims 1 to 29, wherein the 24-hour water uptake of the biocarbon pellet is about 10 wt% or less, measured at 25Â°C and 1 bar. 31. The process of any one of claims 1 to 29, wherein the 24-hour water uptake of the biocarbon pellet is about 6 wt% or less, measured at 25Â°C and 1 bar. 32. The process of any one of claims 1 to 31 , wherein the biocarbon pellet is characterized by a pellet compressive strength at 25Â°C of at least about 100 lbf/in 2 . 33. The process of any one of claims 1 to 32, wherein the biocarbon pellet is characterized by a soaked/dried pellet compressive strength of at least about 100 lbf/in 2 , wherein the soaked/dried pellet compressive strength is measured by soaking the biocarbon pellet in water for 24 hours at 25Â°C, followed by drying the biocarbon pellet in air at 105Â°C for 24 hours. 34. The process of any one of claims 1 to 34, wherein the biocarbon pellet contains no pellet binder other than the polyphenolic material. 35. The process of any one of claims 1 to 35, wherein the biocarbon pellet is subjected to additional pyrolysis to increase the fixed-carbon content of the biocarbon pellet. 36. A process for producing a biocarbon pellet, the process comprising: (a) pyrolyzing a biomass-containing feedstock in a pyrolysis reactor, thereby generating a solid biocarbon-containing material and a pyrolysis vapor that contains a polyphenolic material; (b) introducing the pyrolysis vapor to a thermally controlled multiple-stage separation system configured to separate a pyrolysis condensate in liquid form from non-condensable gases, wherein the pyrolysis condensate contains the polyphenolic material; (c) introducing the solid biocarbon-containing material to a densification unit, thereby generating a first biocarbon pellet precursor; (d) adding the polyphenolic material to the first biocarbon pellet precursor, thereby generating a second biocarbon pellet precursor; and (e) at least partially drying the second biocarbon pellet precursor, thereby generating a biocarbon pellet, 

 wherein the biocarbon pellet is characterized by a 24-hour water uptake of about 20 wt% or less, measured at 25Â°C and 1 bar. 37. A process for producing a biocarbon pellet, the process comprising: (a) pyrolyzing a biomass-containing feedstock in a pyrolysis reactor, thereby generating a solid biocarbon-containing material and a pyrolysis vapor; (b) providing a polyphenolic material; (c) contacting the solid biocarbon-containing material with the polyphenolic material, thereby generating an intermediate mixture; (d) introducing the intermediate mixture to a densification unit, thereby generating a biocarbon pellet precursor; and (e) at least partially drying the biocarbon pellet precursor, thereby generating a biocarbon pellet, wherein the biocarbon pellet is characterized by a 24-hour water uptake of about 20 wt% or less, measured at 25Â°C and 1 bar. 38. The process of claim 37, wherein the biomass-containing feedstock is selected from softwood chips, hardwood chips, timber harvesting residue, tree branches, tree stumps, leaves, bark, sawdust, corn, corn stover, wheat, wheat straw, rice, rice straw, sugarcane, sugarcane bagasse, sugarcane straw, energy cane, sugar beets, sugar beet pulp, sunflowers, sorghum, canola, algae, miscanthus, alfalfa, switchgrass, fruits, fruit shells, fruit stalks, fruit peels, fruit pits, vegetables, vegetable shells, vegetable stalks, vegetable peels, vegetable pits, grape pumice, almond shells, pecan shells, coconut shells, coffee grounds, food waste, commercial waste, grass pellets, hay pellets, wood pellets, cardboard, paper, paper pulp, paper packaging, paper trimmings, food packaging, construction or demolition waste, railroad ties, lignin, animal manure, municipal solid waste, municipal sewage, or a combination thereof. 39. The process of claim 37, wherein the solid biocarbon-containing material contains from about 65 wt% to about 95 wt% fixed carbon. 40. The process of any one of claims 37 to 39, wherein the polyphenolic material contains from about 20 wt% to about 80 wt% fixed carbon. 41. The process of any one of claims 37 to 40, wherein the polyphenolic material has a weight-average molecular weight from about 75 g/mol to about 50,000 g/mol. 42. The process of any one of claims 37 to 41 , wherein the polyphenolic material has a viscosity at 25Â°C from about 1 cP to about 1000 cP. 43. The process of any one of claims 37 to 42, wherein the polyphenolic material contains from about 1 wt% to about 60 wt% water. 44. The process of any one of claims 37 to 43, wherein the intermediate mixture has a weight ratio of the polyphenolic material to the solid biocarbon-containing material selected from about 0.01 to about 5. 45. The process of any one of claims 37 to 44, wherein the densification unit is selected from an extruder, a briquetter, a pellet mill, or a combination thereof. 46. The process of any one of claims 37 to 45, wherein the densification unit is operated at a densification temperature greater than 100Â°C. 47. The process of any one of claims 37 to 46, wherein in step (d), the densification unit is configured to produce an enriched concentration of polyphenolic material in an outer shell of the biocarbon pellet precursor. 48. The process of any one of claims 37 to 47, wherein steps (d) and (e) are combined, and wherein the densification unit is also configured for the drying of the biocarbon pellet precursor. 49. The process of any one of claims 37 to 48, wherein the biocarbon pellet has a moisture content from 0 wt% to about 10 wt% water. 50. The process of any one of claims 37 to 48, wherein the biocarbon pellet has a moisture content from 0 wt% to about 5 wt% water. 51 . The process of any one of claims 37 to 48, wherein the biocarbon pellet has a moisture content from 0 wt% to about 2 wt% water. 52. The process of any one of claims 37 to 51 , wherein the biocarbon pellet has a fixed-carbon content from about 50 wt% fixed carbon to about 99 wt% fixed carbon on a dry basis. 53. The process of any one of claims 37 to 52, wherein the 24-hour water uptake of the biocarbon pellet is about 10 wt% or less, measured at 25Â°C and 1 bar. 54. The process of any one of claims 37 to 52, wherein the 24-hour water uptake of the biocarbon pellet is about 6 wt% or less, measured at 25Â°C and 1 bar. 55. The process of any one of claims 37 to 54, wherein the biocarbon pellet is characterized by a pellet compressive strength at 25Â°C of at least about 100 lbf/in 2 . 56. The process of any one of claims 37 to 55, wherein the biocarbon pellet is characterized by a soaked/dried pellet compressive strength of at least about 100 lbf/in 2 , wherein the soaked/dried pellet compressive strength is measured by soaking the biocarbon pellet in water for 24 hours at 25Â°C, followed by drying the biocarbon pellet in air at 105Â°C for 24 hours. 57. The process of any one of claims 37 to 56, wherein the biocarbon pellet is characterized by a bulk density of at least about 40 lb/ft 3 . 58. The process of any one of claims 37 to 57, wherein the biocarbon pellet contains no pellet binder other than the polyphenolic material. 59. The process of any one of claims 37 to 58, wherein the biocarbon pellet is subjected to additional pyrolysis to increase the fixed-carbon content of the biocarbon pellet. 60. A process for producing a biocarbon pellet, the process comprising: (a) pyrolyzing a biomass-containing feedstock in a pyrolysis reactor, thereby generating a solid biocarbon-containing material and a pyrolysis vapor; (b) providing a polyphenolic material; (c) introducing the solid biocarbon-containing material to a densification unit, thereby generating a first biocarbon pellet precursor; (d) adding the polyphenolic material to the first biocarbon pellet precursor, thereby generating a second biocarbon pellet precursor; and (e) at least partially drying the second biocarbon pellet precursor, thereby generating a biocarbon pellet, wherein the biocarbon pellet is characterized by a 24-hour water uptake of about 20 wt% or less, measured at 25Â°C and 1 bar.
